name: Cheddar-Dill Bread
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: Sue Klapper Preparation Time: 0:40
In a large bowl combine flour and sugar. Cut in butter until crumbly; stir in the cheese and dill. In a small bowl, beat egg and milk; pour into dry ingredients and stir just until moistened. (Batter will be very thick.) Pour into a greased 8 inch x 4 x 2 loaf pan. Bake at 350°F for 35 - 40 minutes or until bread tests done. Cool in pan 10 minutes before removing. Note: As substitute to self-rising flour, place 1 tablespoon baking powder and 1 teaspoon salt in a measuring cup. Add enough flour to equal 2 cups.
Source: Taste of Home; Feb./March 1995.
